channel.title: Haproxy on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Haproxy on Pi Stack

item.title: Self-Hosted Circuit Breaker Patterns: Envoy vs HAProxy vs Linkerd 2026
item.description: When a downstream service starts failing, your entire application can cascade into failure. Circuit breakers prevent this by detecting unhealthy backends and short-circuiting requests before they pile up. Rather than waiting for connections to time out, a circuit breaker trips open, fails fast, and gives the failing service time to recover.

item.title: Self-Hosted DDoS Protection: Nginx vs HAProxy vs BunkerWeb vs CrowdSec 2026
item.description: Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks continue to escalate in both frequency and sophistication. The average attack size grew 28% year-over-year, with application-layer (Layer 7) attacks becoming the most common threat vector for self-hosted services. While cloud providers offer managed DDoS mitigation at a premium, a growing number of open-source tools let you build robust DDoS protection on your own infrastructure.

item.title: Self-Hosted HAProxy Management: Data Plane API vs Prometheus Exporter vs Runtime API 2026
item.description: HAProxy powers millions of production deployments as the world’s most widely-used open-source load balancer and reverse proxy. But managing it at scale — adding backends, checking stats, rotating SSL certs, and monitoring health — requires more than editing a static haproxy.cfg file and sending SIGHUP.
